funder.name: National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke

specimen_procedures.procedure_name: LifeCanvas Active Whole Mouse Brain Delipidation (UNPUBLISHED)

camera.name: Front camera
camera.notes: Max frame rate is at maximum resolution.

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center of the sensor towards the left of the assembly, from the subject's perspective.
device_axes.name: X

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center of the sensor towards the bottom of the assembly.
device_axes.name: Y

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the sensor towards the subject.
device_axes.name: Z

position.device_origin: Located on the face of the lens mounting surface at its center, ie. just ahead of the camera sensor.
position.notes: The rotation matrix is represented as: a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h,i. Wherein a, b, c correspond to the first row of the matrix. The translation matrix is represented as: x,y,z.

camera.name: Side camera
camera.notes: Max frame rate is at maximum resolution.

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center of the sensor towards the left of the assembly, from the subject's perspective.
device_axes.name: X

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center of the sensor towards the bottom of the assembly.
device_axes.name: Y

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the sensor towards the subject.
device_axes.name: Z

position.device_origin: Located on the face of the lens mounting surface at its center, ie. just ahead of the camera sensor.
position.notes: The rotation matrix is represented as: a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h,i. Wherein a, b, c correspond to the first row of the matrix. The translation matrix is represented as: x,y,z.

camera.name: Eye camera
camera.notes: Max frame rate is at maximum resolution.

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center of the sensor towards the left of the assembly, from the subject's perspective.
device_axes.name: X

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center of the sensor towards the bottom of the assembly.
device_axes.name: Y

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the sensor towards the subject.
device_axes.name: Z

position.device_origin: Located on the face of the lens mounting surface at its center, ie. just ahead of the camera sensor.
position.notes: The rotation matrix is represented as: a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h,i. Wherein a, b, c correspond to the first row of the matrix. The translation matrix is represented as: x,y,z.

mouse_platform.name: Mouse Platform
mouse_platform.notes: Radius is the distance from the center of the wheel to the mouse.

patch_cords.name: Patch Cord #1
patch_cords.notes: Numerical aperture is approximately between 0.10 and 0.14.

rig_axes.direction: The world horizontal. Lays on the Mouse Sagittal Plane. Positive direction is towards the nose of the mouse.
rig_axes.name: X

rig_axes.direction: Perpendicular to Y. Positive direction is away from the nose of the mouse.
rig_axes.name: Y

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center towards the right of the screen, from the subject's perspective.
device_axes.name: X

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center towards the top of the screen.
device_axes.name: Y

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the front of the screen towards the subject.
device_axes.name: Z

position.device_origin: Located at the center of the screen. Right and left conventions are relative to the screen side of the monitor, ie. from the subject's perspective.
position.notes: The rotation matrix is represented as: a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h,i. Wherein a, b, c correspond to the first row of the matrix. The translation matrix is represented as: x,y,z.

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center of the speaker towards the right of the speaker, from the subject's perspective.
device_axes.name: X

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the center towards the top of the speaker.
device_axes.name: Y

device_axes.direction: Positive is from the speaker towards the subject.
device_axes.name: Z

position.device_origin: Located on the front mounting flange face. Right and left conventions are relative to the front side of the speaker, ie. from the subject's perspective.
position.notes: The rotation matrix is represented as: a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h,i. Wherein a, b, c correspond to the first row of the matrix. The translation matrix is represented as: x,y,z. Speaker to be mounted with the X axis pointing to the right when viewing the speaker along the Z axis

reward_spouts.name: Reward Spout
reward_spouts.notes: Spout diameter is for inner diameter. Outer diameter is 1.575mm.

metrics.name: Raw data experiment1_ProbeA-AP
metrics.description: This metric displays raw data snippets for both for AP and LFP streams. Check out the snippets for abnormal noise or lack of spikes.Raw data can also be visualized in [Sortingview](https://figurl.org/f?v=npm://@fi-sci/figurl-sortingview@12/dist&d=kachery:aind:sha1:0cbaeb9e3aec98ac5a276f5b78dd476e8fb2ad3a&label=ecephys_834691_2026-02-16_11-02-15%20-%20experiment1_Record%20Node%20101%23Neuropix-PXI-100.ProbeA-AP&zone=aind)

metrics.name: PSD experiment1_ProbeA-AP
metrics.description: This metric displays the power spectral density (PSD) of raw data. PDSs are computed by channel and shown as lines or map (by channel depth). Use the plots to spot contamination from high-frequency or line noise sources.

metrics.name: RMS experiment1_ProbeA-AP
metrics.description: This metric shows the noise levels (RMS) by depth for each channel, computed from the raw and preprocessed data. It also includes a summary of channel labels (after bad channel detection). Use this metric to check if out-of-brain channels are visible (lower RMS values at the top of the probe), but not labeled as 'out'.

metrics.name: Saturation events timeline experiment1_ProbeA-AP
metrics.description: This metric shows a scatter plot of saturation events, with red and blue markers for positive and negative events, respectively. Use this metric to spot an abnormal number of saturation events.

metrics.name: Probe Drift - experiment1_ProbeA-AP
metrics.description: This metric shows a rastermap of the recording with overlaid estimated motion. Use this metric to spot high drifts or bad drift estimation.

metrics.name: Unit Metrics Yield - experiment1_ProbeA-AP
metrics.description: This metric displays a summary of spike sorted units, including distributions of key features and number of units with different labels. Use this metric to report a low yield of units or a disproportionate high number of bad units with respect to good units. The sorting summary can also be viewed in [Sortingview](https://figurl.org/f?v=npm://@fi-sci/figurl-sortingview@12/dist&d=kachery:aind:sha1:2f2f496024306277344117ceaded5193614880db&s={%22sortingCuration%22:%22gh://AllenNeuralDynamics/ephys-sorting-manual-curation/main/ecephys_834691_2026-02-16_11-02-15/experiment1_Record Node 101%23Neuropix-PXI-100.ProbeA-AP/kilosort4/curation.json%22}&label=ecephys_834691_2026-02-16_11-02-15%20-%20experiment1_Record%20Node%20101%23Neuropix-PXI-100.ProbeA-AP%20-%20kilosort4%20-%20Sorting%20Summary&zone=aind)

metrics.name: Firing rate - experiment1_ProbeA-AP
metrics.description: This metric shows the population firing rate across the entire recording. Use this metric to spot abnormal seizure-like activity or firing rate gaps, which could result from hardware failures diring acquisition.

metrics.name: Sorting Curation - experiment1_ProbeA-AP
metrics.description: This metric renders the SpikeInterface GUI through the AIND ephys portal. You can use the GUI to curate spike sorting results (label, remove, merge, split). Use the 'Submit to parent' button to submit the curation data to the QC Portal.

metrics.description: This metric displays raw data snippets for both for AP and LFP streams. Check out the snippets for abnormal noise or lack of spikes.Raw data can also be visualized in [Sortingview](https://figurl.org/f?v=npm://@fi-sci/figurl-sortingview@12/dist&d=kachery:aind:sha1:969d3272d3142a5f7e2d32bb005ce9db88f7334b&label=ecephys_834691_2026-02-16_11-02-15%20-%20experiment1_Record%20Node%20103%23Neuropix-PXI-100.ProbeF-AP&zone=aind)

metrics.name: PSD experiment1_ProbeF-AP
metrics.description: This metric displays the power spectral density (PSD) of raw data. PDSs are computed by channel and shown as lines or map (by channel depth). Use the plots to spot contamination from high-frequency or line noise sources.

metrics.name: RMS experiment1_ProbeF-AP
metrics.description: This metric shows the noise levels (RMS) by depth for each channel, computed from the raw and preprocessed data. It also includes a summary of channel labels (after bad channel detection). Use this metric to check if out-of-brain channels are visible (lower RMS values at the top of the probe), but not labeled as 'out'.

metrics.name: Saturation events timeline experiment1_ProbeF-AP
metrics.description: This metric shows a scatter plot of saturation events, with red and blue markers for positive and negative events, respectively. Use this metric to spot an abnormal number of saturation events.

metrics.name: Probe Drift - experiment1_ProbeF-AP
metrics.description: This metric shows a rastermap of the recording with overlaid estimated motion. Use this metric to spot high drifts or bad drift estimation.

metrics.name: Unit Metrics Yield - experiment1_ProbeF-AP
metrics.description: This metric displays a summary of spike sorted units, including distributions of key features and number of units with different labels. Use this metric to report a low yield of units or a disproportionate high number of bad units with respect to good units. The sorting summary can also be viewed in [Sortingview](https://figurl.org/f?v=npm://@fi-sci/figurl-sortingview@12/dist&d=kachery:aind:sha1:4bb6f7a3a5d284c7becde486cf0dc8514563cff6&s={%22sortingCuration%22:%22gh://AllenNeuralDynamics/ephys-sorting-manual-curation/main/ecephys_834691_2026-02-16_11-02-15/experiment1_Record Node 103%23Neuropix-PXI-100.ProbeF-AP/kilosort4/curation.json%22}&label=ecephys_834691_2026-02-16_11-02-15%20-%20experiment1_Record%20Node%20103%23Neuropix-PXI-100.ProbeF-AP%20-%20kilosort4%20-%20Sorting%20Summary&zone=aind)

metrics.name: Firing rate - experiment1_ProbeF-AP
metrics.description: This metric shows the population firing rate across the entire recording. Use this metric to spot abnormal seizure-like activity or firing rate gaps, which could result from hardware failures diring acquisition.

metrics.name: Sorting Curation - experiment1_ProbeF-AP
metrics.description: This metric renders the SpikeInterface GUI through the AIND ephys portal. You can use the GUI to curate spike sorting results (label, remove, merge, split). Use the 'Submit to parent' button to submit the curation data to the QC Portal.
